title: SecuritySkills Normalized Finding Output
description: Machine-readable envelope for normalized security findings and remediation recommendations emitted by SecuritySkills workflows. This contract is independent of SARIF and may be mapped to downstream enterprise formats later.

schema_version.description: Version of this normalized SecuritySkills finding envelope.

run.description: Metadata for the analysis run that produced this output.

skill.description: Metadata for the SecuritySkills entrypoint that produced this output.

findings.description: Normalized findings produced by a skill run.

id.description: Stable run identifier supplied by the agent, CI job, or enterprise orchestrator.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when the run output was produced.

tool.description: Agent, CLI, scanner, or workflow that executed the skill.

target.description: Repository, project, service, artifact, environment, or other target reviewed.

source_ref.description: Optional commit SHA, branch, image digest, build ID, ticket ID, or evidence package reference.

name.description: SecuritySkills skill name from SKILL.md frontmatter.

version.description: SecuritySkills skill version from SKILL.md frontmatter.

path.description: Path to the SKILL.md entrypoint used for the run.

frameworks.description: Framework identifiers declared by the skill frontmatter.

id.description: Stable finding identifier within the output.

fingerprint.description: Stable deduplication key based on finding type, affected asset, and evidence location.

description.description: What the issue is and why it matters.

framework_refs.description: Framework/control references from the skill frontmatter frameworks.

references.description: Optional advisory, standard, vendor, or knowledge-base references.

location.description: File path, line range, asset ID, log source, policy path, or other concrete evidence location.

snippet.description: Minimal redacted snippet or observation needed to support the finding.

redacted.description: True when sensitive values were intentionally removed from this evidence.

remediations.description: Every fix recommendation for this finding. Each item must include remediation guidance, confidence, blast radius, behavior-change risk, and test strategy.

guidance.description: Concrete remediation steps or patch guidance for the finding.

confidence.description: How confident the skill is that this recommendation is correct for the observed evidence.

blast_radius.description: Expected scope of systems, files, users, integrations, data, or workflows affected by the change.

behavior_change_risk.description: Risk that the remediation changes intended application or operational behavior.

test_strategy.description: Required validation strategy proving the remediation fixes the finding. Include recommended tests, generated tests, or both.

summary.description: Concise explanation of what proves the remediation fixed the issue.

recommended_tests.description: Tests the user or agent should run when tests are not generated directly.

generated_tests.description: Test files or cases produced as part of the remediation.

purpose.description: Behavior, vulnerability condition, or regression this test proves.

command.description: Command or manual check to execute for this test.

path.description: Path to the generated or modified test file.

purpose.description: Behavior, vulnerability condition, or regression this generated test proves.

command.description: Command that runs the generated test.
